name: pysolr (Apache Solr Python Client)

auth.notes: Basic auth or Kerberos (via solrpy). Solr Cloud: ZooKeeper-based.

performance.notes: HTTP-based; Solr performance depends on query complexity and index size

agent_readiness.notes: Python client for Apache Solr search platform. import pysolr. solr = pysolr.Solr('http://localhost:8983/solr/mycore'). solr.add([{'id': '1', 'title': 'Solr doc', 'content': 'full text'}]). solr.commit(). results = solr.search('title:Solr'). for result in results: print(result['title']). results.hits (total). solr.delete(q='id:1'). solr.optimize(). Faceting: solr.search('*:*', **{'facet': 'on', 'facet.field': 'category'}). More Like This, spellcheck, highlighting params. Django Haystack uses pysolr as backend. Solr Cloud: pysolr.ZooKeeper.

gotchas: Apache Solr requires separate Java-based Solr installation and management
Schema must be defined in Solr schema.xml or managed schema
Commit required to make changes searchable (or autoCommit in config)
Complex query syntax uses Solr's Lucene query syntax
Elasticsearch/OpenSearch have largely replaced Solr in new projects
best_when: Existing Solr deployments that need Python integration
Django Haystack applications with Solr backend
When Solr-specific features (faceting, highlighting, MLT) are needed
Enterprise environments already invested in Apache Solr infrastructure
avoid_when: New search projects (use Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, or Typesense instead)
When managed search is needed (no SaaS Solr comparable to Elastic Cloud)
Simple full-text search (use PostgreSQL FTS or SQLite FTS5)
summary: pysolr is the Python client for Apache Solr. import pysolr; solr = pysolr.Solr('http://host:8983/solr/core'); solr.add([{'id': '1', 'title': 'Doc'}]); solr.commit(); results = solr.search('title:query'). Faceting, highlighting. BSD-3. Requires Solr server (Java). Legacy choice — new projects should use Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, or Typesense. Used in Django Haystack.
